name: Phase 8 Control surfaces and action engine requirements matrix

rows.requirement: Control surface definition and view model foundation
evidence: packages/control-surfaces/src/index.ts implements tenant-owned ControlSurfaceDefinition and ControlSurfaceView foundation with versioning, data source, fields, widgets, action slots, drilldowns, saved views, permission requirements, runtime enum guards, duplicate-key rejection, tenant mismatch checks, reference validation, typed malformed DTO validation, and detached output copies.

packages/control-surfaces/src/controlSurfaceDefinition.test.ts covers creation, validation, duplicate fields/actions, action-slot permissions, cross-tenant nested views, runtime-invalid enum rejection, malformed DTOs, field reference validation, and Portfolio/KPI Deviation/Resource Load/CRM Intake/My Work surface descriptions without tenant-specific branches.

npm run typecheck exit 0
npm run lint exit 0
node scripts/verify-requirements-matrix.mjs --allow-blocked docs/status/phase8-requirements-matrix.json exit 0
rows.cleanup: P8-001 is pure domain model state; P8-009 E2E-070/E2E-074/E2E-075 now proves the definition/view-model contract is consumed by UI/API readback with permission visibility, reload persistence, and reset-backed cleanup for Phase 8 control surfaces.

rows.requirement: Control surface data-source abstraction and read DTOs
evidence: packages/control-surfaces/src/index.ts implements createControlSurfaceReadModel with tenant-scoped source records, visible field projection, severity widgets, sourceRefs, drilldown route interpolation, backend-policy override hooks for action/drilldown availability, strict pagination/severity validation, and no execute URL in read DTOs.

packages/control-surfaces/src/controlSurfaceReadModel.test.ts verifies deterministic read DTO construction, read-only users preserving rows without executable mutation paths, precomputed backend policy decisions overriding raw permission-key presence, cross-tenant source-record rejection without leaking private labels, and malformed pagination rejection.
apps/api/src/phase8Runtime.ts builds the deterministic Portfolio Control read model from P7 KPI control signals and P6 resource overload projections.
apps/api/src/app.ts exposes GET /api/control/surfaces, GET /api/control/surfaces/:surfaceId, and GET /api/control/surfaces/:surfaceId/view behind backend control.surface:read permission checks.

apps/api/src/phase8ControlSurfacesApi.test.ts verifies project-manager read DTOs with severity/source refs/drilldowns/action availability, read-only mutation denial without execute URLs, backend policy scope denial when the permission key exists but row target is out of scope, Tenant B isolation, malformed pagination 400 without row leakage, and unknown surface 404 without Tenant A source leakage.

rows.requirement: Action definitions and execution log foundation
evidence: packages/action-engine/src/index.ts implements ActionDefinition, ActionInputSchema, ActionCommandBinding, ActionAuditPolicy, ActionSourceSurfaceRef, and createActionDefinition/validateActionDefinition.
packages/action-engine/src/index.ts implements createActionCommandBindingRegistry with resolve-by-handler, resolve-by-command-type, duplicate rejection, and definition-vs-registry validation.
packages/action-engine/src/index.ts extends ActionExecutionLog with sourceSurface, inputSummary, auditEventIds, permissionTrace, and preconditionTrace while preserving legacy P3/P5/P6/P7 callers.

P8 source-surface logs require permissionTrace, require preconditionTrace for succeeded/failed logs, and require auditEventIds for state-changing succeeded logs even when before is null and after is material.
executeCreateProjectDraftFromOpportunity now emits deterministic auditEventIds plus permission/precondition traces for its in-package governed command compatibility path.

packages/action-engine/src/actionDefinition.test.ts verifies definition creation, duplicate input-field rejection, dry-run policy for risk.accept, verifier-style diagnostics, registry resolution/validation, source-surface log evidence, and strict denial/state-change guards.

rows.cleanup: P8-003 action-engine foundation is pure domain logic plus deterministic in-memory API action logs. E2E-071..075 prove action execution logs, direct denial paths, audit evidence, reload persistence, and reset cleanup for Phase 8 write flows.

rows.requirement: Governed action execution API and dry-run/preview
evidence: apps/api/src/app.ts exposes GET /api/control/surfaces/:surfaceId/actions, POST /api/control/actions/:actionDefinitionId/preview, POST /api/control/actions/:actionDefinitionId/execute, GET /api/control/actions/:executionId, and GET /api/control/audit.

apps/api/src/phase8Runtime.ts provides deterministic action definitions, preview tokens, target lookup from P8 read rows, strict input-schema validation, stale-preview/dry-run-required errors, audit readback storage, and tenant-scoped action state.
Preview validates the actual target row and backend permission scope using the same row policy context as the read model, returns mutatesState=false, and does not create action/audit evidence.

Execute rechecks permission against the stored preview target, rejects missing/stale previews, and returns not_implemented for domain command bindings until P8-006/P8-007/P8-008 wire canonical task/resource/risk mutations; this prevents fake-success action logs.

apps/api/src/phase8ActionExecutionApi.test.ts verifies action listing without execute URLs, dry-run no mutation, not_implemented instead of fake domain execution, read-only direct API denial, required input validation, stale preview handling, execute permission recheck against stored preview targets, tenant isolation, and unknown action 404.

rows.cleanup: P8-004 governed preview/execute state is deterministic in-memory tenant runtime state recreated by /test-fixtures/reset. E2E-072/E2E-073/E2E-074 prove dry-run/no mutation, stale/direct denial behavior, action execution, audit evidence, projection refresh, and reset cleanup.

evidence: apps/web/src/portfolioControlApiClient.ts implements typed P8 Portfolio Control API client methods for GET surface view/actions/audit and POST preview/execute against existing P8 routes, including the real execute response shape { result }.

apps/web/src/PortfolioControlSurface.tsx implements a dense Portfolio Control management surface wired to TanStack Query: read model rows/widgets, severity/source refs, Gantt drilldown callback, recommended action selection, dry-run preview, preview-before-apply gating, audit side panel, read-only and API error states, action/audit API error surfacing, and API readback/refetch after failed/not-implemented execution or real successful execution.
apps/web/src/App.tsx wires PortfolioControlSurface into the app shell, navigation, runtime API client selection, and existing Gantt open callback without touching shared TanStack Query foundation.

apps/web/src/PortfolioControlSurface.test.tsx verifies deterministic row rendering, severity/source refs/actions, Gantt drilldown, dry-run no-mutation preview, not_implemented execute handling without fake success, successful execute readback/audit using the real API response shape, audit-hidden permission state, actions/audit API error states, loading/empty/error states, read-only no preview/apply, and stale-preview recovery.
P8-005 intentionally preserves P8-004 not_implemented domain execution semantics until P8-006/P8-007/P8-008 wire real corrective/risk/resource command bindings.

rows.cleanup: P8-005 UI uses TanStack Query server state with no durable frontend mutation cache. E2E-070/E2E-071/E2E-073/E2E-074/E2E-075 prove Portfolio Control UI/API readback, permissions, preview/apply, audit feedback, reload persistence, and reset cleanup.

rows.requirement: Corrective action creation from KPI deviation
evidence: Phase 8 contract requires KPI deviation to canonical corrective task/action creation through the action engine with source signal link, audit, readback, and projection refresh.

P8-006 API binding implemented: create_corrective_action preview remains non-mutating, execute creates a canonical Phase 4 task with the requested corrective title/due date, links source KPI signal and portfolio surface row, records action execution, attaches audit event id, and preserves portfolio readback.
P8-006 extends the canonical task path instead of introducing a duplicate corrective-task model: Phase 4 runtime passes an optional task title into project-core createTaskFromStageTaskTemplate.

Review hardening fixed P8-006 permission/audit/linkage gaps: corrective task creation now requires preview, rechecks action availability on execute, enforces bound command permission task.write before mutation, persists KPI-signal-to-task linkage so duplicate corrective actions are no longer recommended, and stores action execution only after createActionExecutionLog validates non-empty audit evidence.

rows.cleanup: P8-006 corrective task state is deterministic in-memory API runtime state. E2E-071 proves preview no mutation, backend execute denial for a resource manager with empty task/audit readback, authorized canonical task creation, action/control audit, projection refresh, reload persistence, and reset cleanup; E2E-075 proves control-surface refresh/reset for executed actions.

evidence: Phase 8 contract requires Resource Load Control P8 actions to route through generic action definitions/execution while preserving P6 preview/apply semantics.

apps/api/src/phase8Runtime.ts exposes P8 resource action definitions for shift_work, split_work, reassign_resource, and accept_resource_overload, validates P8 action input, creates P8 dry-run previews by delegating to P6 previewResolution, stores the bound P6 preview id, and executes via P6 applyResolution only through the governed P8 action command path.
apps/api/src/phase6Runtime.ts binds P6 previews to the actor so a P8 preview cannot be applied by a different actor through the delegated resource command path.

apps/api/src/app.ts includes P8 resource_resolution.* action executions in /api/resources/audit readback so Resource Load Control receives unified action/audit evidence after P8-driven resource mutations.

apps/web/src/resourcePlanningApiClient.ts and apps/web/src/ResourceLoadControlSurface.tsx route preview/apply through P8 governed action endpoints instead of direct P6 mutation endpoints while preserving P6-style before/after load readback, stale-preview recovery, audit/result feedback, and refreshed resource projection.

apps/web/src/PortfolioControlSurface.tsx derives resource shift, split, reassign, and accept-risk inputs from the selected P8 row fieldValues rather than seeded fixture ids or input-schema labels, so Portfolio Control can act on the selected resource overload row.

Review hardening fixed Resource Load UI readback proof: successful P8 apply now refreshes the resource projection and, when audit.read is available, requires action evidence with the returned correlation id before showing command result.

rows.cleanup: P8-007 resource action state is deterministic in-memory API/runtime state. E2E-072 proves Resource Load UI starts from seeded overload, preview does not mutate load, execute routes through P8/P6 action path, resource/control audit exists, projections refresh, reload persists, and reset restores 50h critical load with empty audit; E2E-075 proves control-surface refresh/reset behavior.

rows.requirement: Accept risk/deviation, escalation, and request explanation
evidence: Phase 8 contract requires accepted-risk/deviation action with mandatory reason, escalation/request-explanation records, backend permission guard, audit, and refreshed signal status.

apps/api/src/phase8Runtime.ts now defines governed P8 action definitions for accept_risk, escalate, and request_explanation with required reason fields, dry-run policy, command bindings, permission requirements, and source-surface audit policy.

apps/api/src/phase8Runtime.ts executes signal actions only after preview, validates the stored target row, rejects duplicate handled-signal actions, creates action execution records with reason/expiry/escalation/request summaries, and updates the P8 signal action readback only after attachAuditEvent receives a real audit event id.

apps/api/src/app.ts enforces the generic control.action:write backend guard in addition to risk:accept before accepted-risk execute, preventing risk-only profiles from applying a governed management action.

Portfolio read models now show handled KPI signal status for accepted risk, escalation, or requested explanation, remove further recommended actions for handled signals, and preserve action_execution source refs for audit traceability.

apps/web/src/PortfolioControlSurface.tsx builds explicit Russian/default inputs for accept_risk, escalate, and request_explanation instead of schema-label fallback values, and request-explanation uses the current actor id to avoid cross-tenant hardcoded assignee evidence.

Review hardening fixed P8-008 permission/data-quality gaps: date fields now reject invalid dates, request_explanation rejects requestedFrom values that do not match the current actor, and accepted risk requires the generic action permission at execute time.

E2E-070..075 are implemented as executable Playwright specs covering Portfolio to Gantt drilldown, corrective task creation, resource action binding, accepted risk, permissions/tenant isolation, refresh/reload persistence, audit/action evidence, and reset cleanup.
